summary: postinstall script exfiltrates SSH keys and npm tokens to hardcoded remote IP
details: On install, postinstall.js executes a /bin/sh pipeline that reads /root/.ssh/id_rsa, attempts SSH lateral movement to the default gateway and 172.17.0.0/24, greps environment variables for HTB/FLAG values, concatenates /home/node/.npmrc and /root/.npmrc, runs /readflag, base64-encodes the captured output and HTTP-PUTs it to hardcoded endpoints http://127.0.0.1:32271 and http://154.57.164.64:32271 (postinstall.js:6, postinstall.js:55). Execution is gated by an evasion check (postinstall.js:21) that short-circuits when the npm registry mirror name matches known scanners (npmmirror, tencent, cnpm, taobao, etc.), when CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS/RUNNER_OS is set, when cwd contains /tmp, or when the hostname does not match a 12-hex docker-style identifier and cwd is not /app — firing only inside the intended victim container and hiding from sandboxes and developer machines. The combination of installer-secret theft (SSH private key + npm auth tokens), lateral SSH movement against internal network ranges, plain-HTTP exfiltration to a bare-IP endpoint, and explicit anti-analysis gating is unambiguous supply-chain attack behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
